title: Design control flow with pattern matching
description: Match on message and data shape in function heads, letting unexpected shapes crash rather than coding defensive branches.
body: Use when handling messages, parsing data, or branching on shape. Do not write deep nested case/if defensive checks for inputs that should never occur. Actions: pattern-match expected shapes in function heads and handle_* clauses with guards, use the with construct to chain happy-path matches and surface the first failure, and let a truly unexpected shape raise so a supervisor resets state. Validation signals include tests for each matched clause and a confirmed crash-and-restart on an unhandled shape. Risk boundary: matching on data derived from untrusted input must validate it, and matching that silently falls through (a catch-all that swallows errors) reintroduces the defensive sprawl OTP avoids; let-it-crash applies to bugs, not to user-input validation.
